zh.Mitford Castle, Mitford, Northumberland,: 英國諾圖波藍德米特福德米特福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair於大約1080年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德米特福德時，他的父親Sir Richard Bertram36歲，母親Sybella Mitford33歲。他於1125年與Hawise Balliol在英國諾圖波藍德米特福德米特福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1152年之後在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair于大约1080年出生在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德米特福德时，他的父亲Sir Richard Bertram36岁，母亲Sybella Mitford33岁。他于1125年与Hawise Balliol在英国諾圖波藍德米特福德米特福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1152年之后在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair was born about 1080, in Mitford, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Richard Bertram, was 36 and his mother, Sybella Mitford, was 33. He married Hawise Balliol in 1125, in Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died after 1152, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair est né environ 1080, à Mitford, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Richard Bertram, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Sybella Mitford, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Hawise Balliol en 1125, à Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé après 1152, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair um 1080 in Mitford, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard Bertram 36 und seine Mutter Sybella Mitford 33. Er hat 1125 in Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, England Hawise Balliol geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb nach 1152 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair è nato il circa 1080 nel Mitford, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Richard Bertram, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Sybella Mitford, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Hawise Balliol nel 1125, nel Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì dopo il 1152, nel sua cittadina.

ko.lifeSummary: William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair(이)가 대략 1080년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 미트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Bertram(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Sybella Mitford(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1125년 영국 노르스벌랜드 미트퍼드 미트퍼드에서 Hawise Balliol(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1152년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair nasceu aproximadamente 1080, em Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Richard Bertram, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Sybella Mitford, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Hawise Balliol em 1125, em Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu após 1152, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair родился около 1080 г. в местности митфорд, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Bertram было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Sybella Mitford, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise Balliol в 1125 г. в местности митфорд, митфорд, нортумбирланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер после 1152 г. в его родном городе.
